title: verify-registration output
description:
Result of a ``verify-registration`` read — reduced status + per-leg detail.

A REPORT, never a gate: a non-``current`` status is a successful run (the
finding IS the feature). The deployment refusal is wired caller-side against
``status`` (R8). ``view_sha`` is the canonical-JSON sha of ``brief`` — the
witness a subsequent registration sign-off must carry (R6), recomputed by
the gate from the same deterministic projection.

``contested`` / ``prerequisite_contested`` are the C-disclose additions: the
registration's own standing challenges and the per-slot ones. Both are
DISCLOSED, never blocking (C4), orthogonal to ``status`` (C-status), and kept
OUT of the ``view_sha`` projection (R6 — a later challenge must not drift a
bound witness). Absent when nothing is contested (the all-zero omission).

status.description: The reduced registration status (R7): current | stale | revoked | superseded | absent.
status.title: Status

registration_id.description: The resolved registration's id (None when status is absent — no record found).
registration_id.title: Registration Id

registered_at.description: Timestamp the resolved registration was recorded (None when absent).
registered_at.title: Registered At

dossier.description: The dossier-signature leg (None when no registration was resolved).

template.description: The template-drift finding (None when no registration was resolved).

prerequisites.description: Per-slot currency detail for every chain entry (empty when absent).
prerequisites.title: Prerequisites

brief.description: The code-rendered markdown brief the human reviews; its canonical-JSON sha is view_sha.
brief.title: Brief

view_sha.description: Canonical-JSON sha of the rendered brief — the view witness a sign-off must carry (R6).
view_sha.title: View Sha

contested.description: Standing challenges against the registration itself (C-disclose) — counts + ids, orthogonal to status, never blocking. None when uncontested (the all-zero omission).

prerequisite_contested.description: Per-slot standing challenges — one entry per prerequisite whose content_sha is contested (C-disclose). Empty when no prerequisite is contested.
prerequisite_contested.title: Prerequisite Contested

ContestedCounts.description:
The ``contested`` projection every disclosure seat carries (C-status).

Counts and identities, never a severity score (the attention-queue no-urgency
rule; a merit score is core grading dissent). Parallel to — never folded into
— any status vocabulary: a target can be ``current`` AND ``contested``. A
seat whose target has ALL-ZERO counts omits the block entirely (the
emitted-only-when-present precedent); this model is the shape when present.

superseded.description: Count of challenges the target's subject has moved past (computed).
superseded.title: Superseded

challenge_ids.description: The challenge slugs counted here (identities, never a ranking); opaque.
challenge_ids.title: Challenge Ids

ContestedCounts.title: challenge contested counts

DossierLeg.description:
The dossier-subject leg: recorded-vs-recomputed signature + drift (R8).

The registration's subject is the sealed dossier, bound by its
``bundle_sha256`` (R2). ``recomputed_sha`` is the live dry re-gather at read
time; ``recorded_sha`` is what the registration bound. When they differ a
sealed store moved after export — the registration reads ``stale`` and
``drifted_stores`` names the stores whose content moved.

recorded_sha.description: The dossier bundle_sha256 the registration bound.
recorded_sha.title: Recorded Sha

recomputed_sha.description: The dossier bundle_sha256 re-gathered live from the stores at read time.
recomputed_sha.title: Recomputed Sha

drifted_stores.description: Source-store names whose content moved since the dossier was sealed (empty when current).
drifted_stores.title: Drifted Stores

DossierLeg.title: dossier signature leg

FieldsBlock.description:
Template-field completeness by COUNTING (R5) — slugs opaque, never read.

Every declared field slug must have a non-empty value in the registration's
``fields`` (values opaque, never interpreted). This leg reports the declared
slugs, which are present, and which are missing.

declared.description: Field slugs the template declared (opaque caller data).
declared.title: Declared

present.description: Declared slugs that carry a non-empty value in the registration.
present.title: Present

missing.description: Declared slugs with no non-empty value (empty when the registration is complete).
missing.title: Missing

FieldsBlock.title: template fields completeness

PrerequisiteLeg.description:
One prerequisite slot's currency re-evaluated at read time (R8).

Per-slot: the kind's CURRENT condition re-checked. A moved evidence sha is
reported as the ``recorded_sha`` / ``recomputed_sha`` pair (R3 drift-log).
``evidence_note`` echoes what filled the slot — for the generic
``attestation`` kind, the satisfying record's ``{block, attestor}`` verbatim
(R3 disclosure), so an agent-authored record that fills a slot is visible in
the brief, never silent.

slot.description: The chain slot slug (opaque caller data).
slot.title: Slot

kind.description: The mechanism kind this slot was checked through.
kind.title: Kind

status.description: current | stale | absent — the slot's currency at read time.
status.title: Status

recorded_sha.description: The content_sha the entry recorded (None when the evidence is absent).
recorded_sha.title: Recorded Sha

recomputed_sha.description: The sha the kind's checker recomputed at read time (None when absent).
recomputed_sha.title: Recomputed Sha

evidence_note.description: What filled the slot, echoed verbatim (e.g. the attestation kind's {block, attestor}); or the shortfall cause when absent. Opaque, echoed, never parsed.
evidence_note.title: Evidence Note

PrerequisiteLeg.title: prerequisite slot leg

SlotContested.description:
One prerequisite slot that has standing challenges against its content_sha.

C-disclose: a ``contested`` block per prerequisite leg whose ``content_sha`` is
challenged. Only slots WITH standing challenges appear (the emitted-only-when-
present precedent); ``contested`` is orthogonal to the leg's ``status`` — a
``current`` prerequisite may be ``contested`` (never-blocking, C4). Kept OUT of
the ``PrerequisiteLeg`` (and thus out of ``view_sha``) so a challenge filed
later never drifts a bound witness (R6).

slot.description: The chain slot slug whose content_sha is contested (opaque).
slot.title: Slot

contested.description: The standing-challenge counts + ids against this slot's content_sha.

SlotContested.title: prerequisite slot contested block

TemplateLeg.description:
The template-drift finding (R5) — always disclosed, never a silent gate.

Template drift does NOT retroactively revoke a registration (the subject is
the dossier; the template's raw-bytes sha is recorded on the record). This
leg REPORTS ``current | stale`` so a consumer can require re-registration
under new standards — the drift is disclosed, the consumer decides.

status.description: current = the on-disk template matches the recorded sha; stale = it drifted.
status.title: Status

recorded_sha.description: The template raw-bytes sha the registration recorded.
recorded_sha.title: Recorded Sha

recomputed_sha.description: The template file's raw-bytes sha on disk at read time.
recomputed_sha.title: Recomputed Sha

TemplateLeg.title: template drift leg
